channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news and analysis from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: China and Philippines Trade Accusations Over Disputed South China Sea Reef
item.description: China accused the Philippines of landing personnel on a disputed reef while Manila said it would dispatch ships to drive off Chinese vessels conducting illegal research. The latest escalation in territorial tensions comes amid ongoing disputes in the strategic waterway.

item.title: Israel Approves Multi-Billion Dollar Fighter Jet Purchase from US
item.description: Israel gave final approval to purchase two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA advanced fighter aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing. The deal, worth tens of billions of dollars, significantly strengthens Israeli air power amid regional tensions.

item.title: Trump Warns US Could Restart Iran Strikes If Tehran Misbehaves
item.description: President Trump said the US could resume strikes on Iran if the regime misbehaves, while expressing dissatisfaction with Irans proposed 14-point peace deal. Trump said he prefers a non-military path but remains skeptical of Tehrans intentions.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine Give Conflicting Accounts of Sumy Region Fighting
item.description: Russias Defence Ministry claimed its troops took control of Myropillia village in Ukraines Sumy region, but Ukraines military denied the village had changed hands. The conflicting reports highlight ongoing intense fighting along the northeastern front.

item.title: Rubio to Visit Vatican and Italy After Trump-Pope Tensions
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to the Vatican and Italy for meetings this week, following President Trump's public attacks on Pope Leo that drew criticism from Christians across the political spectrum.

item.title: Hezbollah Pays Steep Price as Israel Dismantles Its Strongholds
item.description: Israel has displaced hundreds of thousands of Hezbollah's Shi'ite constituents and killed thousands of its fighters, while domestic rivals harden opposition to its status as an armed group.

item.title: Slovakia PM Fico to Attend Victory Day Parade in Moscow
item.description: Prime Minister Robert Fico is among a handful of global leaders attending Russia's scaled-back Victory Day parade, highlighting divisions within Europe over engagement with Moscow.

item.title: OPEC+ Agrees Third Oil Output Hike Since Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ will agree to a modest oil output hike, but the increase will remain largely symbolic as the US-Iran war continues to disrupt Gulf oil supplies through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: North Korea Rejects US Cyber Threat Claims as Fabrication
item.description: Pyongyang's foreign ministry dismissed US accusations of cyber threats as disinformation aimed at justifying Washington's hostile policy, warning of countermeasures.

item.title: Russia faces worker shortage crisis as war depletes labor force
item.description: Russia has run out of workers for the first time in its post-Soviet history, with record-low 2.1% unemployment driven by military conscription, emigration, and war casualties.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes on Tuapse refinery cause major oil spill disaster
item.description: Hundreds of tons of oil leaked into the Black Sea after Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia's Tuapse refinery, creating an ecological disaster. Russian authorities have been accused of covering up the extent of the damage.

item.title: Saudi Arabia and UAE lead push for Hormuz-bypass trade corridors
item.description: Middle Eastern states are dusting off proposals for overland pipelines and rail-sea corridors to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, building a new trade arc connecting the Gulf to global markets without transiting the Iranian-controlled chokepoint.

item.title: Taiwan President Defies China with Surprise Eswatini Visit
item.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te arrived in Eswatini for a surprise diplomatic visit, asserting Taiwan right to engage with the world despite Beijing calling him a rat and trying to block the trip.

item.title: Two Dead as Migrant Boat Runs Aground in France
item.description: A small boat carrying 82 migrants ran aground on a beach in northern France, leaving two women dead and 16 injured. This is the third deadly incident this year involving migrants attempting to cross the English Channel.

item.title: Israel Approves Purchase of F-35 and F-15IA Fighter Jet Squadrons
item.description: Israel gave final approval to purchase two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA fighter aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing, part of a $119 billion deal that significantly expands Israeli air capabilities.

item.title: Oil Tanker Hijacked Off Yemen Coast Near Somalia
item.description: An oil tanker was hijacked off the coast of Yemen and taken towards Somalia, marking the second such incident in the past 10 days amid ongoing regional instability.

item.title: Cuba Condemns New US Sanctions as Illegal and Abusive
item.description: Cuba denounced new US sanctions as illegal and abusive, adding to existing US oil blockade measures that have caused widespread blackouts and fuel shortages on the island.

item.title: NATO seeks answers from US on Germany troop cuts
item.description: NATO says it is working with the US to understand details of planned troop reductions in Germany, after Trump announced deeper cuts beyond the initial 5,000 troop drawdown.

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian Baltic port with drone swarm
item.description: Ukrainian drones hit Primorsk oil terminal near St Petersburg, marking a major escalation as Ukraine extends its reach deep into Russian territory. The strike temporarily set the port on fire.

item.title: Germany Mittelstand shows resilience amid industrial decline
item.description: Small and midsize enterprises (SMEs) remain the backbone of Germanys economy, with hidden champions like Zeiss and Herrenknecht maintaining global leadership despite broader industrial challenges.

item.title: Myanmar military gains ground but civil war far from over
item.description: The junta has retaken territory and opened political channels after rigged elections installed Min Aung Hlaing as president, but resistance groups still control significant parts of the country.

item.title: Bangkok street food culture under threat from vendor crackdown
item.description: Thailands capital is relocating street vendors from busy districts, raising concerns about livelihoods and the iconic food culture that defines Bangkok.

item.title: New Zealand PM Luxon visits Singapore for annual leaders meeting
item.description: Christopher Luxons three-day visit focuses on strengthening the New Zealand-Singapore Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, with agreements on trade in green economy and clean energy.

item.title: Americas energy dominance strategy faces long-term risks
item.description: A Straits Times analysis argues that Trumps approach to energy geopolitics - from sanctions to military action in Iran - may undermine US oil industry fundamentals and global energy stability.

item.title: China issues injunction blocking US sanctions on five refineries
item.description: Chinas Commerce Ministry moved to block US sanctions on five Chinese refiners accused of buying Iranian oil, issuing an injunction through state news agency Xinhua.
